name: Galicia (Galicia / Galiza)
summary: Galicia (Galicia in Spanish / Galiza in Galician) is a North-western Iberian Atlantic-coastal civil-law autonomous community of the Kingdom of Spain — structurally distinctive globally as the only EU member-state subnational jurisdiction with a Celtic-heritage-recognised constitutional identity (Galician one of the seven recognised Celtic nations per the Celtic League framework) operating its own Foral Civil Law tradition, and as the only EU subnational with Atlantic-rural-economy-derived distinctive civil-law institutions (compañía familiar gallega 'Galician family company' framework, vitalicio life-tenancy framework, derecho de troncalidad rural-land-succession framework distinctive to Galician rural-economic-tradition). Galicia's distinct civil-law tradition (Dereito Civil de Galicia) derives from the medieval Foros de Galicia (~13th-15th centuries), was preserved through the 1880 Royal Order (despite abolition of fueros politically), formally compiled in the 1963 Compilation of Special Civil Law of Galicia (Compilación del Derecho Civil Especial de Galicia), comprehensively recodified as the Lei 2/2006 de dereito civil de Galicia (Galician Civil Law Act 2006) effective 19 July 2006. Family-law framework operates under Lei 2/2006 Title IV (Family Law) covering parental authority (patria potestade), child custody (garda e custodia), and family-property regime — including the distinctive sociedade de gananciais galega framework (Galician community-property variant). Galicia operates the Concierto Económico framework via the unified Spanish federal taxation system (unlike Basque Country and Navarre fiscal-autonomy framework) — making Galicia the largest civil-foral-law autonomous community without full fiscal autonomy. The Tribunal Superior de Xustiza de Galicia (TSXG) is the apex appellate court for Galician-civil-law matters; final appellate jurisdiction on Spanish constitutional questions lies with the Spanish Constitutional Court. Galicia is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label. Spain is a Hague Convention 1980 party (acceded 16 June 1987) — Galician Hague applicability via Spanish federal extension subject to Galician Foral Civil Law application framework.

statutory_framework.citation: Lei 2/2006 de dereito civil de Galicia (Galician Civil Law Act 2006)
statutory_framework.title: Galician Civil Law Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Galician Civil Law Act effective 19 July 2006 — comprehensive recodification of Galician foral civil law. Title IV (Family Law) covers parental authority, child custody, and family-property regime including sociedade de gananciais galega.

statutory_framework.title: Statute of Autonomy of Galicia
statutory_framework.relevance: Spanish Organic Law establishing Galician autonomous-community governance framework with distinct civil-law jurisdiction preservation.

statutory_framework.citation: Compilación del Derecho Civil Especial de Galicia 1963 (replaced by Lei 2/2006)
statutory_framework.title: Compilation of Special Civil Law of Galicia 1963
statutory_framework.relevance: Predecessor compilation of Galician civil law adopted 2 December 1963 — replaced by Lei 2/2006 de dereito civil de Galicia.

statutory_framework.title: Spanish Constitution Article 149.1.8
statutory_framework.relevance: Spanish Constitution providing for federal-level competence over civil legislation except in autonomous communities with preserved foral or special civil law — constitutional foundation for Galician civil-law jurisdiction.

apex_courts.name: Tribunal Superior de Xustiza de Galicia (TSXG)
apex_courts.role: Apex appellate court for Galician-foral-civil-law matters from Galicia.

apex_courts.name: Tribunal Constitucional de España
apex_courts.role: Apex Spanish constitutional court for federal constitutional questions from Galicia.

professional_regulators.name: Colexio Oficial de Psicoloxía de Galicia
professional_regulators.role: Galician autonomous-community professional regulatory framework for psychology.

anonymisation_convention: Galician family-court decisions are anonymised per Galician court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Suebic Kingdom of Galicia + early-medieval-Celtic-heritage framework
key_developments.description: Suebic Kingdom of Galicia 410-585 CE — substantively distinctive North-western Iberian early-medieval-Germanic-kingdom-with-Celtic-substrate framework. Foundational pre-1287-Foros-de-Galicia framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory and Celtic-heritage-recognised constitutional identity.

key_developments.title: Foro Real + Foros-de-Galicia + customary-law-foundational framework
key_developments.description: Foro Real granted to Galicia ~13th century — substantively distinctive North-western Iberian Foros-de-Galicia framework. Foundational substantive Galician customary-law framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory affecting family-law-implementation including compañía-familiar-gallega + vitalicio + derecho-de-troncalidad rural-economy framework.

key_developments.title: Junta del Reino de Galicia + medieval-Galician-self-governance framework
key_developments.description: Junta del Reino de Galicia established 1481 — substantively distinctive North-western Iberian medieval-Galician-self-governance framework. Subsequent dissolution 1834 with Spanish provincial reorganisation. Foundational pre-1880-Royal-Order framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Royal Order + Galician-foral-civil-law-preservation framework
key_developments.description: Spanish Royal Order of 1880 preserving Galician foral civil law despite abolition of political fueros — substantively distinctive North-western Iberian Galician-foral-civil-law-preservation framework. Foundational substantive Galician-civil-law-preservation framework persisting through subsequent 1963 Compilación reform-trajectory affecting family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: Galician Statute of Autonomy 1936 + Second-Spanish-Republic + suspended-by-Civil-War framework
key_developments.description: Galician Statute of Autonomy 1936 approved by 28 June 1936 referendum during Second Spanish Republic — substantively distinctive globally suspended-by-Civil-War-+-restored-only-in-1981 framework. Subsequent suspension by Francoist regime through 1981. Foundational pre-1981-Statute-of-Autonomy framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Compilación del Derecho Civil Especial de Galicia + first-modern-formal-codification framework
key_developments.description: Compilación del Derecho Civil Especial de Galicia adopted 2 December 1963 — substantively significant North-western Iberian first-modern-formal-codification framework. Foundational substantive Galician-civil-law-codification framework persisting through subsequent 2006 Lei 2/2006 replacement affecting family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: Spanish Constitution Article 149.1.8 + foral-civil-law-preservation-constitutional foundation framework
key_developments.description: Spanish Constitution 1978 Article 149.1.8 providing for foral civil law preservation — substantively significant constitutional foundation for Galician civil-law jurisdiction. Foundational substantive constitutional anchor for contemporary family-law jurisprudence affecting subsequent 1981 Statute of Autonomy + 2006 Lei 2/2006 reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Statute of Autonomy of Galicia + Spanish-Organic-Law framework
key_developments.description: Spanish Organic Law of 6 April 1981 establishing Galician autonomous-community governance framework with distinct civil-law jurisdiction preservation — substantively distinctive North-western Iberian Galician-autonomous-community-+-Celtic-heritage-recognised-constitutional-identity framework. Foundational substantive constitutional anchor for contemporary family-law jurisprudence.

key_developments.title: Lei 2/2006 de dereito civil de Galicia + comprehensive-recodification + Title-IV-Family-Law framework
key_developments.description: Galician Civil Law Act effective 19 July 2006 — substantively significant North-western Iberian comprehensive-recodification framework of Galician foral civil law replacing 1963 Compilation. Title IV (Family Law) covers parental authority (patria potestade), child custody (garda e custodia), and family-property regime including sociedade de gananciais galega community-property variant. Foundational substantive Galician-civil-law framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Rueda PP Government + Parlamento-de-Galicia + Valedor-do-Pobo + Camino-de-Santiago framework
key_developments.description: Alfonso Rueda (PP) Presidency framework continuing through 2023 with Parlamento de Galicia institutional positions addressing PA-related issues within Lei 2/2006 de dereito civil de Galicia Title IV (Family Law) framework. Valedor do Pobo (Galician Ombudsperson) institutional positions affecting cross-border family-law adjudication. Continuing Camino de Santiago framework + Galician-Portuguese cross-border family-law adjudication framework substantively significant institutional context. Substantively significant North-western Iberian Galician-foral-civil-law institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Tribunal Superior de Xustiza de Galicia + Tribunal Constitucional — Galician-foral-civil-law substantive register + continuing-autonomous-community framework
key_developments.description: Tribunal Superior de Xustiza de Galicia (TSXG) and Tribunal Constitucional de España continue to develop Galician-foral-civil-law jurisprudence under Lei 2/2006 Title IV (Family Law) + Spanish Constitution Article 149.1.8 + Hague Convention 1980 framework in custody and family-property disputes within continuing Galician autonomous-community framework. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Galicia operates a civil-law framework with own Galician foral civil-law jurisdiction — places Galicia in the EU subnational civil-law-with-own-code cluster.
Only EU member-state subnational jurisdiction with Celtic-heritage-recognised constitutional identity operating its own Foral Civil Law tradition is structurally distinctive globally.

Only EU subnational with Atlantic-rural-economy-derived distinctive civil-law institutions (compañía familiar gallega, vitalicio, derecho de troncalidad rural-land-succession) is structurally distinctive globally.
Lei 2/2006 de dereito civil de Galicia is structurally distinctive within European subnational civil-code recodification cluster.

Largest civil-foral-law Spanish autonomous community without full fiscal autonomy is structurally distinctive within Iberian foral cluster — distinct from Basque Concierto Económico and Navarrese Convenio Económico.
Sociedade de gananciais galega (Galician community-property variant) is structurally distinctive within European community-property cluster.
Spanish Constitution Article 149.1.8 (foral civil law preservation) applies via the Galician-civil-law framework.
Spanish Hague Convention 1980 accession 1987 + Galician Foral Civil Law application framework is structurally distinctive.

sources.title: Tribunal Superior de Xustiza de Galicia

editorial_notes: Galicia jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 5 to 10 key_developments with full Suebic-Kingdom-of-Galicia-410CE-to-continuing-autonomous-community trajectory: 410-Suebic-Kingdom-of-Galicia + 1287-Foro-Real-+-Foros-de-Galicia + 1481-Junta-del-Reino-de-Galicia + 1880-Royal-Order-preserving-foral-civil-law + 1936-Galician-Statute-of-Autonomy-suspended-by-Civil-War + 1963-Compilación + 1978-Spanish-Constitution-Article-149.1.8 + 1981-Statute-of-Autonomy + 2006-Lei-2/2006-de-dereito-civil-de-Galicia + 2024-TSXG-+-Tribunal-Constitucional-Galician-foral-civil-law.

Civil-law North-western Iberian Atlantic-coastal Spanish autonomous community with own Galician foral civil-law jurisdiction (Lei 2/2006 de dereito civil de Galicia + Statute of Autonomy 1981 + Compilación del Derecho Civil Especial de Galicia 1963 + Spanish Constitution Article 149.1.8 + Spanish Hague Convention 1980 accession 1987). Only EU member-state subnational with Celtic-heritage-recognised constitutional identity operating own Foral Civil Law tradition + only EU subnational with Atlantic-rural-economy-derived distinctive civil-law institutions (compañía familiar gallega / vitalicio / derecho de troncalidad) + largest civil-foral-law Spanish autonomous community without full fiscal autonomy + sociedade de gananciais galega community-property variant. Completes Iberian foral civil-law quintet (Galicia + Aragon + Catalonia + Basque + Navarre).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive Galician-foral-civil-law analysis under Lei 2/2006 Title IV (Family Law) + Spanish Constitution Article 149.1.8 + Hague Convention 1980 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins North-western-Iberian + Atlantic-coastal + civil-law + EU-subnational-civil-law-with-own-code-cluster + Celtic-heritage-recognised-constitutional-identity-globally-distinctive-one-of-seven-Celtic-nations + Atlantic-rural-economy-distinctive-civil-law-institutions-globally-distinctive + compañía-familiar-gallega + vitalicio + derecho-de-troncalidad-rural-succession + sociedade-de-gananciais-galega-community-property-variant + Suebic-Kingdom-of-Galicia-410-585CE + Foros-de-Galicia-Foro-Real-1287 + Junta-del-Reino-de-Galicia-1481-1834 + Spanish-Royal-Order-1880-foral-civil-law-preservation + Galician-Statute-of-Autonomy-1936-suspended-by-Civil-War-distinctive + Compilación-1963 + Lei-2/2006-de-dereito-civil-de-Galicia + largest-civil-foral-law-Spanish-autonomous-community-without-full-fiscal-autonomy + Iberian-foral-civil-law-quintet-Galicia-+-Aragon-+-Catalonia-+-Basque-+-Navarre clusters within the corpus.
